description_long: This object consists of five distinct clusters of flowering plants, each with upright green stems and vibrant blossoms. The flowers are arranged in vertical rows, with each cluster displaying a different color: blue, red, or white. The blossoms have a radial symmetry, with multiple petals radiating outward from a central core, and the petals are slightly overlapping, creating a layered effect. The leaves are lanceolate, narrow, and pointed, growing alternately along the stems. The overall appearance is delicate and ornamental, suitable for decorative planting in urban parks, sidewalks, or gardens. The flowers are positioned at the terminal ends of the stems, making them prominent and visually striking.
description: A group of five upright flowering plants with blue, red, and white blossoms, lanceolate leaves, and terminally positioned flowers. The clusters are visually vibrant and arranged in neat rows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the vertical alignment of the five flower clusters, with clear visibility of the blue, red, and white blossoms.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the spacing between each flower cluster and the alternating arrangement of leaves along the stems.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back sides of the flower clusters and the density of the foliage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the flower clusters from a different angle, emphasizing the radial symmetry of the blossoms.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, radiating symmetrically from the center of each flower. The petals are slightly overlapping and layered, forming a whorled pattern typical of many ornamental flowers.
